comment.body:
LISTEN,
Whenever you take a longer-term position (monthly or longer), always assume your profits.
But when you calculate your expected profits, always take into account the
1) Bid/ask spread 
2) Fees
3) The taxes you will pay for that profit
4) Inflation (estimate the future value of your profit)
+1) check how much % could have been earned through treassury bonds (your country, tax-free), to check the risk-free profit

So, whenever you take a trade, you already in loss. And you need to take into account these to determine your target zones for exits and size-ups

comment.body:
Also don't chase tops and bottoms.
Market is a stochastic system. All we have is a probability distribution, that can be derived from the options positioning.

Catching tops and bottoms may look virtuoso, but thats not the point of trading. 
It is not about to play some sort of "guess the top" games, but to win more than lose. 
Loss is a part of the game. 
Place your bets, if you have a read. Determine the size through your confidence level. And when the move is confirmed by the structure, size up. If it is disproved, cut the loss as soon as possible. There is no point to sit on losses if the market doesn't want to give it to you. 

(You saw me catching tops and bottoms many times, but not always. My strategy was always to determine the area for the reversion, and go for it, but never all-in, but scale in, if I have conviction. These areas were very near to tops and bottoms, sure, but I do this for mor than a decade, using algos and very accurate datas, and the main thing: my motivation was NEVER to catch the tops or bottoms. As the market is stochastic, whenever you catch it, it is only by fortune, nothing more. Everything else is furu lie. Take it)

comment.body:
Alma... in recent days i reread some of the april and may chats, both critiques and analyses, and posts to look back on that period from a bit of distance. I could feel your tears behind your words and sensed how emotionally taxing all the information you’d gathered had been..as you tried to piece together the big picture while struggling with your fears. I also saw that this chat didn’t help..in fact, it left you more overwhelmed. I fully understand that you wanted to share everything you’d found and heard. Among the many details (many of which were often indeed unnecessary), you still had your bottom calls and pivot calls, just as you do now. But in the noise many people lost sight of them.
The subscribers’ frustration was understandable too. It showed that this was your first time doing something like this. I mean, producing content for hundreds or even thousands of people, and that you needed to get comfortable with it as you went.
I know that several people unsubscribed because of this content and your emotionally charged defensive reactions, and that they’re now using these weaknesses against you. I’m a successful manager, but I’ve never created this kind of content.. I can see and feel that it’s a completely different challenge than providing analyses for a company. Many don’t understand this. You’re a woman, and as i understand, something of a lone wolf, so this kind of stress is an extra challenge for you, not to mention the pressure of having to perform every day in front of two thousand people.
Still, I see that you’ve successfully conquered your demons, and your writings have become very polished. The quality is as high as ever, but the presentation is much more professional and clear. You’ve returned to the chat, and subscribers visibly feel good here again. I believe you’re on the right path.
I know you’re doing this alone and may not have someone to discuss many of your thoughts with, so you must distinguish what to share with these two thousand people and what to keep back. You’re on the right path, Alma.

I’m very sorry that many good guys left and that some are dancing with joy over your past weaknesses and trying to discredit you. I really hope they will see your new posts and return to you..after all, they subscribed to you once almost without thinking. I hope as many people as possible recognize the value you represent and that the old subscribers forgive you.

comment.body:
And one more thing.. i know many attack you by claiming that you aren’t really a woman. I don’t know that. You might be a woman, a cosplay guy, or transsexual, as some allege..i don’t know, and it’s none of my business. True, you posted a trans flag during pride, and in that picture you looked beautiful in your pajama top. But as I said, it’s not my business.
What drew me (and everyone who follows you) is your soul, your deep reflections, and your spirit‑filled charisma. You’re full of knowledge, humility, and love for the world and its people. You’ve given us so much for free and at a heavily discounted rate here. You’re the only analyst who can simultaneously make sense of volatility, volume profile, momentum, and macro analysis in a truly predictive way. You don’t just talk about gamma or trendlines being broken.. you deliver hf level analyses for pennies..often even more direct than a hf quant desk.
No one else explains markets like you do, combining mechanical flow, mathematical rigor, and simple sentiment aspects. You do this instinctively. Everyone sees it.Even those attacking you. Their attacks are just marketing ploys to serve their own ends.
So whoever you are, you haven’t deceived anyone.. rather, you modestly share your immense knowledge and talent with us. For that, i’m very grateful, and i know many others are too. I hope people discover you as soon as possible. 
We’ll help with that.
And i hope those insider friends who might have wanted to exploit or harm you didn’t actually mean to. If they did, DM me freely. We’ll take care of them.

comment.body:
A lot of wisdom from 0mik here, and pills that are big to swallow. So much care ❤️.
 I would add:

 drop all reference to gender, including cute pics, giving airtime of gender comments etc. Proving we're this or that is intensely distracting from the magic of maths, stochastics, and financial mastery in the posts.
 Our greatest women financial folks have noooooothing to prove, and it shows. They bang out phenomenal financials, and by giving absolutely no air time to gender issues (although real, and hurtful when we're on the receiving end) you stop giving it power. As the saying goes, "Be a lion among foxes." It's not easy, and we're here for you. Loving watching you level up!
